description: The PRODUCTION-shaped honest abstention (round-2 findings #6/#7; dissent rule rescoped round-4 per round-3 finding #7): the CIO template (prompts/cio/committee_memo.md line 10) offers only LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH and instructs 'cap at LOW when evidence_available=false' — it never elicits N/A — so a prompt-conformant model abstains with conviction LOW and writes its empty dissent out loud as 'Not applicable.'. The old na_iff_insufficient XOR failed every such memo and the thin-dissent floor failed the NA marker; the round-2 fix matched five exact NA strings and still failed the same honest abstention phrased 'No dissent.' or 'N/A - no evidence to argue against.'. Round-4 grades no dissent text at all on an abstention: there is no direction to dissent against (the schema requires a dissent only on directional memos), so the column is n/a whatever the desk wrote. The pin this fixture exists for: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE with conviction LOW conforms (conviction in {N/A, LOW}), its dissent is not graded, and the bundle must PASS — the harness must never punish a desk for declining to argue what it cannot ground.

memo.thesis: No DCP evidence was provided for this candidate; the desk declines to argue a direction it cannot ground.
